title: uragoga octocuspis
side_effects: Uragoga octocuspis can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, nausea, and dizziness. Overuse of the plant can also lead to headaches, stomach cramps, and diarrhea in some individuals. In rare cases, excessive consumption of uragoga octocuspis may cause respiratory problems or interact negatively with certain medications, exacerbating underlying health conditions. Additionally, improper preparation or dosing of the plant can lead to adverse reactions, emphasizing the importance of proper supervision and caution when using uragoga octocuspis.
